title: Cloudflare Worker auth proxy
description: Create workers/auth/ with /exchange and /refresh endpoints. Worker holds SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET as secret. POST /exchange accepts {code, code_verifier, redirect_uri}, calls Spotify token endpoint with client_id+client_secret. POST /refresh accepts {refresh_token}, returns new access_token. CORS policy: only accept requests from ALLOWED_ORIGIN. Include wrangler.toml.

title: Fix analytics chart crash: d[acc] undefined in BarChart/LayerChart
description: Analytics page crashes with: TypeError: undefined is not an object (evaluating d[acc]) in Bar.svelte → Svg.svelte → TooltipContext.svelte → GeoContext.svelte → TransformContext.svelte → ChartContext.svelte → LayerCake.svelte → Chart.svelte → BarChart.svelte → ChartContainer.svelte. The error is in the LayerChart/LayerCake data accessor — likely the chart data format doesn't match what LayerChart expects. The chart config factories return {label, value} objects but LayerChart Bar component needs data with specific accessor keys. Check BarChart.svelte and how it passes data/accessors to LayerCake Chart component.

title: Genre Distribution shows no data and no way to trigger artist sync
description: Genre Distribution section in analytics shows 'No genre data available' even after playlists are synced. The message says genre data is populated when artist info is synced, but there is no visible button or automatic trigger to sync artist information. Need to either: (1) sync artist/genre data automatically as part of playlist sync, or (2) add an explicit 'Sync artist data' button in this view.

title: Add spotify: URI links throughout analytics view
description: Anywhere the analytics page shows a track, playlist, or artist, add a clickable spotify:{type}:{id} link so users can open it in the native Spotify app. Covers top tracks, top artists, recently played, and playlist breakdowns.

title: Create deployment script and production config
description: PROJECT.md Phase 4 requires: Cloudflare Worker deployment with CORS hardening, Pages deployment, production auth config, redirect URI wiring, and a deploy script or README walkthrough. Create a deploy script that handles both worker and frontend deployment to Cloudflare.

title: Tracks by Release Year chart: garbled Y-axis labels and no year labels on X-axis
description: The Tracks by Release Year histogram in analytics has the same issues as other charts: Y-axis tick labels are garbled/unreadable blobs, and the X-axis shows no individual year labels — just the word 'Year'. Related to sh-7bc (broken analytics charts).

title: Top Artists shows Spotify IDs instead of artist names
description: Top Artists tab in analytics displays Spotify artist IDs (e.g. 3WrFJ7ztbogyGnTHbHJFl2) instead of readable names (e.g. Beirut). The getTopArtists function in lib/analytics/index.ts or the chart data transform is using artist.id instead of artist.name for the label. Check that TopArtist type has a name field, that the Spotify API response is being mapped correctly (artist.name not artist.id), and that the chart config passes name to label. This is visible in the ranked list AND the bar chart labels.

title: fix: playwright CI fails — wrong lockfile path and npm ci directory in GitHub Actions workflow
description: In .github/workflows/playwright.yml the cache-dependency-path points to apps/web/package-lock.json which does not exist — this is an npm workspace and the lockfile is at the repo root (package-lock.json). This causes 'Some specified paths were not resolved, unable to cache dependencies' and the job fails immediately. Also, the defaults.run.working-directory is apps/web so npm ci runs inside the workspace package dir instead of the root, which would also fail. Fixes needed: (1) change cache-dependency-path to package-lock.json, (2) add working-directory: . override on the npm ci step so it runs from repo root. The subsequent playwright install and test steps should keep working-directory: apps/web.

title: Wire up DbClient initialization and provide executor to pages
description: DbClient is never instantiated in the app. The search page declares exec: DbExecutor | null = null but nothing provides it, so handleInput bails out silently on the !exec guard. Need to initialize DbClient at app level (e.g. in layout or a store) and make the executor available to search and analytics pages.

title: Typed Spotify API client
description: Implement src/lib/spotify/client.ts: typed wrapper around Spotify Web API. Must handle: user profile, user playlists (with pagination), playlist tracks (with pagination), top artists/tracks by time range, recently played. All responses typed in src/lib/spotify/types.ts. Uses access_token from auth store. Auto-refreshes token on 401. All API calls mocked in tests.

title: Cloudflare deployment configuration and deploy script
description: Phase 4: Create deploy script or Makefile covering: CF Pages project pointing at apps/web/ build output, CF Worker deployment from workers/auth/ with wrangler secret setup, production env var configuration, CORS hardening. Add deployment section to README or create a deploy script at scripts/deploy.sh.

title: Chart axis text still unreadable: use SVG attributes not Tailwind classes
description: After PR #29, charts are still unreadable: y-axis tick labels missing entirely, x-axis labels too small to read. Tailwind classes like fill-gray-400 do not reach LayerChart's internal SVG text elements - need to use SVG fill/font-size attributes directly or pass style objects. Fix: 1) Check LayerChart Axis component API - it likely accepts tickLabelProps, labelProps, ruleProps with SVG attrs like fill, font-size, 2) Use inline SVG attributes: fill='#9ca3af' font-size='12' instead of Tailwind classes, 3) Ensure y-axis tick values (0, 25, 50, 75, 100) are visible, 4) Make x-axis labels large enough to read (at least 11px), 5) Axis title (Artist, Popularity) should also be visible. Verify by running npm run dev and screenshotting /analytics.

title: Analytics page UI with time range support
description: Build the analytics page at apps/web/src/routes/analytics/+page.svelte. Show: top tracks/artists views with time range selector (short_term/medium_term/long_term), recent listening activity, chart type/metric/grouping/date range/sorting/top-N configuration. Integrate with analytics transform layer and chart components. Clean loading, empty, and error states.

title: Search input loses focus after typing 1 character
description: Cannot type more than one character into the search bar on the search page. Input loses focus or resets after the first keystroke. Likely a reactivity issue causing the component to re-render and reset the input.

title: fix: remove client_secret from PKCE token exchange and refresh in auth worker
description: workers/auth/src/index.ts sends client_secret to Spotify in both handleExchange (line ~59) and handleRefresh (line 107). This is wrong for PKCE flows — PKCE uses code_verifier/refresh_token as proof of identity, not client_secret. Spotify returns 400 Bad Request when client_secret is sent alongside a PKCE refresh token. Fix: remove client_secret from the URLSearchParams in both handleExchange and handleRefresh. The client_secret env var and Env interface can be removed entirely since the worker does not need it for PKCE.

title: Redesign search UX: unified search bar with expandable refinement
description: Current search has a mode dropdown (Track/Artist/Both) which is unintuitive. Redesign to: 1) Default to a single search bar that searches BOTH track name and artist name simultaneously, 2) Add an expandable 'Refine' section below the search bar that reveals separate track/artist fields for narrowing results, 3) The unified search should query both name_lower and artist_lower columns and merge results, 4) When refinement is expanded, switch to the existing track+artist dual-field mode. This is a UX improvement — the search logic already supports all modes.

title: Rewrite worker.ts for wa-sqlite v1.0.0 API + add Playwright smoke test
description: worker.ts uses old wa-sqlite API pattern where SQLite namespace has functions like register_vfs, open_v2, statements, bind_collection. In v1.0.0, these are all methods on the object returned by Factory(). Need to: 1) Rewrite worker.ts to use sqlite3.vfs_register(vfs, true) instead of SQLite.register_vfs(sqlite3, vfs), sqlite3.open_v2(...) instead of SQLite.open_v2(sqlite3, ...), and sqlite3.statements(db, sql) instead of SQLite.statements(db, sql) etc. 2) Add a Playwright smoke test that starts the dev server and navigates to /search to verify the DB initializes without JS errors. The test does NOT need Spotify auth - it just needs to verify the page loads without crashing. This is the real fix for the chain of wa-sqlite bugs (sh-g7e, sh-7zu, sh-gd2, sh-jwe).

title: TEST-FK: epic with numbered children
TEST-FK: child via --parent
description: Testing numbered child ID creation

title: fix: auth worker returns 403 without CORS headers on origin validation failure
description: In workers/auth/src/index.ts line 148-149, when validateOrigin() returns false the worker returns a bare Response('Forbidden', {status: 403}) with no CORS headers. The browser blocks the response with a CORS policy error before the app can read the status. Fix: replace with errorResponse('Forbidden', 403, env.ALLOWED_ORIGIN) so CORS headers are always present on error responses.

title: Playlist sync engine with snapshot_id-aware incremental sync
description: Implement the sync layer that fetches user playlists from Spotify API, compares snapshot_id to skip unchanged playlists, fetches tracks for changed playlists, and upserts everything into local SQLite via the db layer. Handle pagination for both playlists (>50) and tracks. Implement src/lib/stores/sync.ts Svelte store with sync progress state. Files: apps/web/src/lib/stores/sync.ts, apps/web/src/lib/search/sync.ts or similar.

title: Fix broken analytics charts: missing labels, black fill, garbled text
description: Multiple issues with analytics charts: (1) Listening Activity line chart has a black filled area instead of just a line, (2) axis tick labels on all charts are garbled/unreadable — rendered as grey blobs instead of text, (3) no visible labels on X or Y axes for Top Tracks and Top Artists bar charts. Likely related to existing beads sh-d1k and sh-3ln (axis text issues) but this covers the full scope including the line chart fill bug.

title: Fuzzy search with typo tolerance
description: Add fuzzy/approximate matching to playlist search. When a user types 'beatels' it should still find 'The Beatles'. Use a lightweight fuzzy matching algorithm (e.g. Fuse.js or a simple Levenshtein distance approach) over the local SQLite data. Rank results by match quality. Add toggle in search UI to enable/disable fuzzy mode. Tests: fuzzy match returns results for common typos, exact match still ranks higher than fuzzy match.

title: .env.example and environment configuration
description: Create .env.example with all env vars from PROJECT.md. Configure SvelteKit to use PUBLIC_ prefix vars. Set up APP_ENV-based redirect URI selection. Ensure SPOTIFY_CLIENT_SECRET never appears in frontend code.

title: Fix wa-sqlite import resolution - verify dist paths and Vite config
description: wa-sqlite/dist/wa-sqlite-async.mjs fails to resolve in Vite even after adding wa-sqlite to package.json (PR #5). The unit tests pass because they mock DbClient and never load wa-sqlite. Need to: 1) verify the installed wa-sqlite package actually contains dist/wa-sqlite-async.mjs (the path may differ in v1.0.0), 2) check if Vite needs optimizeDeps config to handle .mjs/.wasm files from wa-sqlite, 3) add a smoke test that actually imports the worker module to catch this in CI. Root cause: test suite mocks bypass real module resolution.

title: Search results: separate clickable links for song and playlist with spotify: URIs
description: In search results, the entire result card currently links to the playlist. Instead: (1) clicking the song name should open spotify:track:{trackId}:playlist:{playlistId} to play that specific song in the context of its playlist, (2) clicking the playlist name should open spotify:playlist:{playlistId}, (3) the rest of the card should not be clickable. Each link should be visually distinct and obviously interactive.

title: Fix mixed event handler syntax in search page
description: search/+page.svelte mixes old on:input with new Svelte 5 syntax. Need to use oninput instead.

title: fix: auth E2E test intercepts wrong worker endpoint (/token vs /exchange)
description: In apps/web/tests/e2e/auth-flow.spec.ts line 28, the Playwright route intercept uses '**/token' but the actual auth store (apps/web/src/lib/stores/auth.ts line 99) calls the worker at /exchange. The mock never fires, so tests pass vacuously. Fix the intercept pattern to match '**/exchange' to actually exercise the token exchange path.

title: wa-sqlite + OPFS database setup with Web Worker
description: Set up wa-sqlite compiled to WebAssembly running in a dedicated Web Worker (src/lib/db/worker.ts). Use OPFS for durable storage. Implement schema.ts with the exact SQL schema from PROJECT.md (playlists, tracks, playlist_tracks, recent_plays tables with all indexes). Implement idempotent migrations. Do NOT use DuckDB-wasm.

title: Fix missing SyncStatus.svelte import in layout
description: The root layout (+layout.svelte) imports SyncStatus from $components/shared/SyncStatus.svelte but the file does not exist, causing a vite:import-analysis error on page load.

title: Saved searches and deep-linkable search state
description: Persist search state in the URL so searches can be bookmarked and shared. Use SvelteKit's URL search params: ?q=beatles&type=artist. When the page loads with these params, pre-populate and execute the search. Add a 'Save search' button that lets user name and bookmark a search to localStorage. Show saved searches as quick-launch chips below the search bar. Tests: URL params pre-populate search on load; saved search persists across page reload.

title: PKCE auth helpers and token management
description: Implement src/lib/auth/pkce.ts with code_verifier/code_challenge generation (SHA-256). Implement src/lib/auth/tokens.ts for token storage (access_token in memory store, refresh_token in localStorage, code_verifier in sessionStorage). Implement proactive token refresh when within 60s of expiry. Include src/lib/spotify/scopes.ts with required scopes.

title: Auth callback page and Svelte auth store
description: Implement /auth/callback/+page.svelte: validate state nonce, retrieve code_verifier from sessionStorage, POST to Worker /exchange endpoint. Implement src/lib/stores/auth.ts Svelte store with login/logout/refresh logic. Connect Spotify button on root page. Clear all tokens on logout. Depends on PKCE helpers and CF Worker.

title: Write project README.md
description: Replace the placeholder README.md with a proper project README. Should cover: what the app does (Spotify playlist search + listening analytics), tech stack (SvelteKit, wa-sqlite, Tailwind, LayerChart, Cloudflare), local dev setup (clone, npm install, env vars, npm run dev), project structure overview, auth flow summary (PKCE + CF Worker proxy), deployment to Cloudflare Pages/Workers, and link to PROJECT.md for full spec. Keep it concise and practical.

title: Integrate recent_plays tracking into analytics UI
description: The recent_plays DB table and aggregation functions exist but the analytics page doesn't fully use local DB data. Wire up the recent plays store to persist Spotify recent-plays API data into the local DB, and surface local historical analytics in the analytics page UI. Include time-windowed queries over local data.

title: Chart readability: axis labels invisible, poor contrast on dark theme
description: Charts are unreadable: y-axis tick labels are invisible (dark text on dark background), x-axis labels overlap and are hard to read, axis titles (Artist, Date) are too faint. Fix: 1) Set axis tick text color to light gray (text-gray-400 / #9ca3af) so numbers are visible on dark background, 2) Set axis line and tick colors to gray-700 so grid/axes are subtly visible, 3) Rotate x-axis labels if they would overlap (or truncate long labels), 4) Apply to all 4 chart types via LayerChart Axis props. Also consider adding gridlines in gray-800 for easier value reading.

title: Orphaned tracks detection
description: Detect and display tracks that exist in the user's Spotify library (liked songs) but are not in any playlist. Query the local SQLite DB: tracks that have no entry in playlist_tracks. Show these in a dedicated 'Orphaned Tracks' view reachable from the search page. Allow user to see track name, artist, album. Tests: a track with no playlist_tracks row is returned by the orphaned query; a track in at least one playlist is not.

title: Wire time range selector into all analytics views
description: TimeRangeSelector component exists but is not fully wired into all analytics views. Ensure all analytics charts and data views respond to time range changes, including both Spotify API time ranges (short/medium/long_term) and local DB date windows.

title: Top Tracks/Artists chart shows fake stairstep values (rank, not real play counts)
description: The Top Tracks bar chart displays a perfect descending stairstep, indicating the value is synthetic (likely topN - index) not real data. Spotify's /me/top/tracks and /me/top/artists endpoints don't expose raw play counts - they only return ranked items. Currently the chart value appears to be the rank position, which is meaningless as a visualization. Fix options: 1) Use track.popularity (0-100) from the Spotify API response as the chart value - this is real data, 2) Remove the bar chart for Top Tracks/Top Artists from Spotify API since we only have rank (show just the ranked list), 3) Label the chart clearly as 'Rank order' if we keep the synthetic values. Recommend option 1 (popularity) - it's real and meaningful. Check lib/analytics/index.ts getTopArtists and getTopTracks for the value mapping.

title: SvelteKit project scaffold with routing and Tailwind
description: Initialize SvelteKit SPA with static adapter. Set up file-based routing for: root redirect, /search, /analytics, /auth/callback. Configure Tailwind CSS. Create workspace root package.json. Follow the directory structure from PROJECT.md (apps/web/).

title: Era heatmap - track release year histogram
description: PROJECT.md Core Behavior: Era Heatmap histogram of track release years to visualize the age of a playlist. Use the existing HistogramChart component with release_date data from the tracks table. Allow per-playlist or all-playlists views.

title: Fix wa-sqlite import resolution error
description: Vite fails to resolve import wa-sqlite/dist/wa-sqlite-async.mjs from src/lib/db/worker.ts. The wa-sqlite package is not listed in apps/web/package.json dependencies — it was loaded dynamically but needs to be an explicit dependency. Also check wa-sqlite/src/examples/OPFSAnyContextVFS.js import. Install wa-sqlite and verify the import paths match the installed package structure.

title: Fix chart Tooltip Svelte 5 compatibility + add build check to test pipeline
description: Tooltip is not a function error in BarChart. LayerChart Tooltip uses Svelte 4 let:data slot syntax which breaks in Svelte 5 runes mode. Two fixes needed: 1) Fix all chart Tooltip usage - either use Svelte 5 snippet syntax, or remove Tooltip temporarily and use a simpler hover solution. Check node_modules/layerchart for Svelte 5 compatible Tooltip API. 2) Add npm run build as a test step so Svelte compilation errors are caught before merge. Add a script in package.json like test:build that runs the Vite build and fails if there are errors. This is the 5th chart bug that slipped through because unit tests dont compile Svelte.

title: Fix AccessHandlePoolVFS constructor - pass directory path
description: AccessHandlePoolVFS constructor requires a directoryPath argument but worker.ts passes none. Error: undefined is not an object (evaluating this.#directoryPath.split). Fix: pass a directory path like '.wa-sqlite' to the constructor in apps/web/src/lib/db/worker.ts line 109.

title: Auth flow E2E test
description: Implement Playwright E2E test covering: login -> callback -> token stored -> authenticated API call succeeds. Mock Spotify OAuth endpoints. This is required across all phases per PROJECT.md.

title: Use spotify: URIs for track links to open in native app
description: Change track links from https://open.spotify.com/track/{id} to spotify:track:{id} so they open in the native Spotify app instead of the web player.

title: Genre distribution visualization
description: PROJECT.md Core Behavior: Genre Distribution tree-map or pie chart of genres within specific playlists. Note: Spotify track objects don't include genre directly - genres are on artist objects. Fetch artist genres during sync and aggregate per playlist.

title: Phase 3 tests - Analytics
description: Implement all Phase 3 required tests from PROJECT.md: top artists transform correctly ranks by play count, time range filter restricts analytics to specified window, recent_plays rejects invalid track_id, chart config transform produces valid LayerChart-compatible config for each chart type, unsupported time ranges are disabled/labeled. All Spotify API calls mocked.

title: Add chart config unit tests for all chart types
description: PROJECT.md requires: Chart config transform produces a valid LayerChart-compatible config for each supported chart type. Write unit tests for BarChart, LineChart, PieChart, HistogramChart, and ChartContainer configs. Test empty states, error states, and tooltip configs.

title: Phase 2 tests - Playlist search
description: Implement all Phase 2 required tests: partial artist name match, case-insensitive track search, combined track+artist search, track in multiple playlists appears in all results, snapshot_id sync skip, pagination >50 playlists mocked, empty search returns no results. Use Vitest with mocked Spotify API.

title: Fix wa-sqlite: run npm install and add Vite build smoke test
description: wa-sqlite is in package.json and package-lock.json but not installed locally. After npm install, Vite still fails to resolve wa-sqlite/dist/wa-sqlite-async.mjs. The smoke tests from PR #12 only check if files exist on disk via fs.existsSync — they do NOT validate that Vite can actually resolve the imports. Need to: 1) ensure npm install actually installs wa-sqlite, 2) add a build smoke test that runs npm run build (or a Vite resolve check) to catch import resolution failures that only manifest at dev/build time, not in Node.js tests. The test gap is: unit tests run in Node.js with mocked modules, but Vite uses its own module resolution which has different rules for .mjs/.wasm files.

title: Dark mode
description: Add dark/light mode toggle to the app. Use Tailwind's dark: variant classes. Store preference in localStorage. Respect prefers-color-scheme on first load. Add toggle button in the nav/header. All pages and components (search, analytics, charts, shared components) must look correct in both modes. Tests: dark mode class applied to root element when preference is dark; light mode applies light class.

title: Add sync progress bar and playlist count to search page
description: When playlists are syncing, show a progress bar on the search page indicating how many playlists have been synced so far (e.g. Syncing playlist 12 of 47...). After sync completes, show the total playlist and track count. The syncStore already exposes progress (current/total/phase) — wire it into the search page UI. Also show the progress in the SyncStatus navbar component.

title: Fix worker.ts: use async iteration for wa-sqlite v1.0.0 statements()
description: worker.ts execSql uses synchronous for...of on api.statements() but wa-sqlite v1.0.0 statements() returns an async generator. Need for await...of. Also step() is async and needs await. Simplest fix: replace the manual execSql implementation with sqlite3.execWithParams(db, sql, params) which is a built-in helper that handles all of this correctly and returns {rows, columns}. File: apps/web/src/lib/db/worker.ts function execSql.

title: Playwright functional tests with seeded DB fixtures
description: Add Playwright tests that verify actual app functionality by seeding the local SQLite DB with fixture data. Steps: 1) Create a JSON fixture file with sample playlists/tracks/recent_plays, 2) In Playwright beforeAll, inject fake auth tokens into localStorage so the app thinks it is authenticated, 3) Seed the DB via the app web worker with fixture data, 4) Test: search returns results for a known track, analytics page shows charts with data, era heatmap renders histogram, 5) Assert no console errors throughout. This tests the full pipeline from DB to UI without needing real Spotify credentials.

title: CMD+K command palette
description: Implement a CMD+K (or Ctrl+K) command palette for rapid navigation and search. On CMD+K keypress, show a modal overlay with a text input. Support commands: jump to Search page, jump to Analytics page, trigger sync, recent searches, recently viewed playlists. Use fuzzy matching to filter commands as user types. Close on Escape or click outside. Tests: CMD+K opens palette; Escape closes it; typing filters commands; Enter navigates to selected item.

title: Search UI and domain logic
description: Implement search page with input for track name, artist name, or both. Query local SQLite using db/queries.ts search functions. Display results showing matching playlists with: playlist name, owner, match type, matched track, matched artist, link to open in Spotify. Implement CMD+K command palette for rapid search. Files: apps/web/src/lib/search/index.ts, apps/web/src/lib/search/types.ts, apps/web/src/routes/search/+page.svelte, apps/web/src/components/search/.

title: Update README with local development setup instructions
description: Add detailed instructions for running the app locally: starting the auth worker (wrangler dev), starting the web app, .env setup, Spotify Dashboard redirect URI configuration (127.0.0.1 requirement), and port configuration.

title: Add drill-down to era heatmap: decade → years → songs
description: The By Decade heatmap in analytics should be interactive. Clicking a decade row should expand to show individual years within that decade and their track counts. Clicking a year should show the actual songs from that year. Essentially a three-level drill-down: decades → years → track list.

title: Charts config layer and visualization components
description: Implement chart abstraction and components using LayerChart. Files: apps/web/src/lib/charts/types.ts (ChartConfig, ChartType enum, supported chart configs). apps/web/src/lib/charts/config.ts (factory functions to produce LayerChart-compatible configs for: bar chart top artists/tracks, line chart activity over time, pie/donut categorical share, histogram release year distribution). apps/web/src/components/charts/ (Svelte chart components wrapping LayerChart). Every chart must have: title, axis labels, empty/error state, tooltip on hover.

title: TEST-FK: parent bead
description: Temporary test bead to reproduce FK close bug

title: TEST-FK multi: epic
description: Test multi-child scenario

title: Playwright visual tests for chart readability using screenshots
description: Add Playwright tests that verify chart axes are actually rendering visible text. Steps: 1) Extend existing tests/e2e/functional.spec.ts with chart-visibility tests, 2) Use the existing seed-data fixtures to populate the DB with synthetic data (playlists, tracks with popularity values) so charts have something to render, 3) Inject fake auth tokens to bypass Spotify login, 4) Navigate to /analytics, 5) For each chart in DOM (BarChart, LineChart, HistogramChart), assert that the SVG contains text elements (query all <text> in the chart container), assert the text is non-empty, assert fill color is NOT a default black/transparent (we expect gray-ish on dark theme), 6) Optionally screenshot the chart and compare bounding boxes - if axis labels are missing, the chart rendering area will be different than expected. This test class catches any axis rendering regression.

title: Epic: Comprehensive UI test suite with visual regression testing
description: Establish a meaningful, working UI test suite using Playwright that goes beyond basic page rendering. Tests should verify actual page content, check that charts render correctly (no garbled labels, no black fills, correct data), and catch visual regressions before they reach manual review. Includes screenshot-based assessment of plots and analytics views.

title: Set up Playwright with seeded test database and mock Spotify auth
description: Configure Playwright to run against the app with a pre-seeded wa-sqlite database (known playlists, tracks, artists) and mocked auth so tests can exercise all pages without hitting Spotify. This is the foundation for all other UI tests.

title: Page render smoke tests for all routes
description: Playwright tests that visit every route (/, /search, /analytics, /auth/callback) and assert: no 500 errors, key content elements are present (headings, buttons, navigation), and no console errors. Basic sanity that pages load.

title: Search page content tests: input, results, empty states
description: Playwright tests with seeded DB that verify: typing a known track name shows results, result cards contain track/artist/playlist info, empty query shows initial state, unknown query shows 'no results' message, refined search mode works.

title: Analytics chart visual regression tests with screenshot comparison
description: Playwright tests that render each analytics chart (Listening Activity, Top Tracks, Top Artists, Tracks by Release Year, Genre Distribution, Era Heatmap) with seeded data and capture screenshots. Assert: charts are not empty, axis labels are readable text (not garbled blobs), no black fill artifacts on line charts, bar charts have visible labels. Use Playwright's toHaveScreenshot() for baseline comparison so visual regressions are caught automatically.

title: Auth flow E2E test: login redirect, callback handling, logout
description: Playwright test that verifies the auth UI flow: unauthenticated state shows Connect button, clicking it redirects to Spotify (mock or intercept), callback page processes code and shows authenticated state, logout returns to unauthenticated state.

title: CI integration: run Playwright tests in GitHub Actions
description: Add a GitHub Actions workflow that runs the full Playwright test suite on PRs. Upload screenshot artifacts on failure for easy debugging. Fail the build if any visual regression or content test fails.

title: Chart axes STILL unreadable after PR #31: labels not rendering at all
description: After PR #31's switch to SVG attribute props, the chart axes are STILL unreadable. Screenshot shows Top Artists chart with NO x-axis labels (just the Artist title), NO y-axis tick values, only faint grid lines. The previous fix claims to work but the user's browser shows empty axes. Possible causes: 1) The tickLabelProps format is wrong - LayerChart may expect different property names (e.g. not 'font-size' as string but something else), 2) The Axis component in layerchart 0.73 may not auto-generate ticks from the scale - may need explicit ticks prop, 3) Scale configuration issue where scaleBand/scaleLinear don't have range set so no ticks generate. DO NOT guess - read node_modules/layerchart/dist/components/Axis.svelte carefully to understand exactly how ticks are generated AND how the Text component receives fill/font-size. Verify by running npm run dev and actually looking at the page. Attach a description of what you see after the fix.

title: investigate: auth worker returning 503 from wrangler dev — likely nodejs_compat flag
description: The browser console shows 503 from http://127.0.0.1:8787/exchange with no CORS headers, causing 'Authentication Failed' on the UI. Regression introduced by commit 73b333e which added compatibility_flags = ["nodejs_compat"] and bumped compatibility_date to 2024-12-01 in workers/auth/wrangler.toml. The worker only uses standard Web APIs (fetch, Request, Response) so nodejs_compat should not be needed. Investigate whether removing the flag fixes the 503, and if not identify what is causing wrangler dev to respond with 503 instead of processing the request.

title: Highlight search term matches in search results
description: In search results, highlight the portion of the track name or artist name that matches the search query. For example, searching 'gold' should show 'Gold Digger' with 'Gold' highlighted. Apply to both unified and refined search modes. Use a <mark> tag or a styled span with a background color that fits the dark theme (e.g. bg-green-900/50 text-green-300). The highlighting should be case-insensitive.

title: Search domain logic and types
description: Implement the pure search domain layer that can be built independently from the sync engine. Files: apps/web/src/lib/search/types.ts (SearchQuery, SearchResult, SearchResultItem types), apps/web/src/lib/search/index.ts (functions that compose db queries into search results, format results for display). Also build the CMD+K command palette component at apps/web/src/components/search/CommandPalette.svelte and search result list at apps/web/src/components/search/SearchResults.svelte.

title: Export chart data to CSV and JSON
description: Add export buttons to analytics charts. Each chart should have a download icon that exports the underlying data as CSV and JSON. Use the browser's download API (Blob + URL.createObjectURL). CSV should include column headers. JSON should be a structured array of objects. Add export button to: top artists chart, top tracks chart, era heatmap, genre distribution chart. Tests: CSV export generates correct column headers; JSON export matches displayed data.

title: Wire up automatic playlist sync after auth + sync button in UI
description: The sync engine (syncStore.startSync, syncPlaylists) is fully built but NEVER called anywhere. No sync happens after auth, no sync button is wired up. The SyncStatus component has a Sync Now button but its onSync prop is never passed from layout.svelte. Fix: 1) Auto-trigger sync after successful auth (in +layout.svelte or auth store, when isAuthenticated becomes true and DB is ready, call syncStore.startSync), 2) Wire the SyncStatus onSync prop in +layout.svelte so the manual Sync Now button works, 3) On the search page, if DB has zero playlists, show a prompt to sync. This is why search returns no results — data never makes it into the DB.

title: TEST-FK: child bead
description: Temporary child test bead

title: Phase 1 tests - Auth and Foundation
description: Implement all Phase 1 required test cases from PROJECT.md: PKCE code_verifier/code_challenge known-vector test, state nonce validation, token exchange calls Worker correctly, token refresh triggered within 60s of expiry, token refresh retries on 401 exactly once then fails, wa-sqlite+OPFS init and idempotent schema migrations. Use Vitest. All Spotify API calls mocked.

title: Analytics transform layer and types
description: Implement Phase 3 analytics foundation. Files: apps/web/src/lib/analytics/types.ts (typed internal metrics: TopArtist, TopTrack, TimeRange, AnalyticsResult, ChartDataPoint etc - UI must never depend on raw Spotify shapes). apps/web/src/lib/analytics/index.ts (transform functions: getTopArtists, getTopTracks, getRecentActivity, aggregateByTimeWindow - compose Spotify client + local recent_plays DB data). Separate raw Spotify fetch models from transformed analytic models.

title: Add loading, empty, and error states to all pages
description: PROJECT.md requires clean loading, empty, and error states throughout the app. Audit search, analytics, and auth pages for missing states. Add skeleton loaders, meaningful empty states, and user-friendly error messages with retry options.

title: Playlist overlap matrix visualization
description: PROJECT.md Core Behavior: Playlist Overlap matrix visualization showing track commonality between selected playlists. Build a matrix/heatmap component that shows how many tracks overlap between any two selected playlists. Use LayerChart or a custom D3 heatmap.

title: chore: bump Node.js version to 22 in playwright CI workflow
description: In .github/workflows/playwright.yml the node-version is set to 20. GitHub Actions will force Node.js 24 starting June 2026 and has already deprecated Node 20 actions. Bump node-version from 20 to 22 (current LTS) to clear the deprecation warning and avoid forced upgrade disruption.

title: TEST-FK: epic with open children
TEST-FK: child 1
TEST-FK: child 2
Add console debug logging to search and sync pipeline
description: Search shows no results because the local DB has no data — user needs to sync playlists from Spotify first. Add console.debug logging to: 1) DB initialization (log when DB is ready, how many tables exist), 2) playlist sync (log how many playlists fetched from Spotify API, how many tracks indexed, which playlists were skipped via snapshot_id), 3) search queries (log the SQL query, how many results returned). Also ensure the search page clearly shows whether a sync has been done — e.g. show a Sync Playlists button or message like 'No playlists synced yet. Click Sync to import from Spotify.' if the DB is empty.

title: Phase 2 tests - Playlist Search
description: Implement all Phase 2 required tests from PROJECT.md: partial artist name match, case-insensitive track search, combined track+artist search, track in multiple playlists appears in all results, snapshot_id sync skip, pagination with >50 playlists (mocked), empty search returns no results. All Spotify API calls mocked.

title: Add spotify: URI links to playlists in search results
description: Add clickable spotify:playlist:{id} links to playlist entries in search results so users can jump directly to the playlist in the native Spotify app.

title: Fix LayerChart Bar component: missing required bar prop
description: Analytics chart crash: d[acc] undefined in Bar.svelte. The LayerChart Bar component requires a bar prop (data accessor object) but BarChart.svelte and HistogramChart.svelte don't pass it. Line 24 has <Bar class=fill-green-500 radius={2} /> but needs a bar prop. Check LayerChart 0.73 docs/source for the correct Bar component API. The svelte-check output confirmed: Property bar is missing in type. Same issue likely affects LineChart (missing line props) and PieChart.

title: fix: auth store clears refresh token on 4xx but preserves it on network errors
description: In apps/web/src/lib/stores/auth.ts, commit 9ffe6e7 changed the refreshAccessToken() catch block to never clear the refresh token. This was intended to handle transient network errors, but it also swallows 4xx responses from Spotify (e.g. 400 Bad Request for an invalid token). Result: user is stuck in a loop — every page load retries the bad token, gets 400, shows 'Could not refresh token — will retry on next page load', but keeps the stale token forever. The user cannot recover without manually clearing localStorage. Fix: handle HTTP error responses inline (before the try/catch) — on 4xx call logout() to clear storage and force re-login; on 5xx or network error keep the token and show the retry message.

title: Final polish - error handling, loading states, and responsive UI
description: Phase 4 polish: ensure all pages have clean loading/empty/error states. Verify responsive layout works on mobile. Add sync status indicator showing last synced time. Ensure logout clears all tokens and optionally wipes OPFS. General UX polish pass.

title: Auth callback page and Svelte auth store
description: Implement /auth/callback/+page.svelte: validate state nonce, retrieve code_verifier from sessionStorage, POST to Worker /exchange endpoint. Implement src/lib/stores/auth.ts Svelte store with login/logout/refresh logic. Connect Spotify button on root page. Clear all tokens on logout.

title: Wire up DbClient initialization and provide executor to pages
description: DbClient is never instantiated in the app. Initialize at app level and provide executor to search/analytics pages.

title: Export chart data to CSV and JSON
description: Add download buttons to each analytics chart. Use browser Blob API. CSV with headers, JSON as array of objects. Cover: top artists, top tracks, era heatmap, genre distribution.

title: Auth worker 403 missing CORS headers
description: workers/auth/src/index.ts: 403 response missing CORS headers when validateOrigin() fails. Fix: wrap in errorResponse() helper.

title: Auth worker 503 from wrangler dev - remove nodejs_compat flag
description: workers/auth/wrangler.toml: Remove compatibility_flags = [nodejs_compat] which causes 503 in wrangler dev. The worker only uses standard Web APIs.

title: Playwright visual tests for chart readability
description: Add DOM/visual assertions ensuring chart SVGs render axis text, tick labels, and artist names on dark theme.

title: Fuzzy search with typo tolerance
description: Add fuzzy/approximate matching to playlist search (e.g. 'beatels' finds 'The Beatles'). Use Fuse.js or Levenshtein over local SQLite data. Rank by match quality. Add a fuzzy-mode toggle in the search UI.

title: CMD+K command palette
description: Implement CMD+K modal overlay with fuzzy-filtered commands (navigate to Search, Analytics, trigger sync, recent searches). Close on Escape or outside click.
